source.sourcePath: raw/sources/RA/04_제품별 인허가 진행 문서/04_2G & 3G (A1417MCW, A1717MCW, F1417MCW)/▶ CE MDR/230926 기술문서 & 임상평가 보완 리뷰 2 (with 컨설팅)/CE MDR TD 보완_230926/A-TCF-5-157 Clinical Evaluation/비교제품 근거자료/4.1 CXDI-31 Service manual.pdf

sourceSummary.oneLine: Canon CXDI-31 Service Manual Ver.06, dated Jun. 2009, covering service, installation, safety, labeling, specifications, standards, and parts information for the CXDI-31 digital X-ray camera system.
sourceSummary.officialUse: After-sales service guide for installation, repair, maintenance, parts ordering, safety precautions, regulatory labels, and image quality/network setup of the Canon CXDI-31.
keyPoints: The manual is published by Canon Inc. as part of its product quality guarantee program and is confidential Canon property.
The CXDI-31 is identified as a digital X-ray camera with imaging unit, E/O box, optical composite cable, power cable, storage box, and document set.
For U.S.A., the equipment is described as complying with Class A digital device limits pursuant to FCC Part 15.
For EU Countries, the instrument is marked for compliance with Directive 93/42/EEC and classified under EN55011, EN 60601-1, and EN60825-1:1994.
Specifications include a 226 mm x 288 mm effective filming range, 2256 x 2878 pixel matrix, about 6.5 million pixels, 100 μm pixel size, and DICOM3.0 Ethernet interface via control PC.

evidence.claim: The document is the CXDI-31 Service Manual, Ver.06, dated Jun. 2009.
evidence.evidenceText: CXDI-31 Service Manual Ver.06 ... Jun. 2009

evidence.claim: The product name identified in the manual is CXDI-31.
evidence.evidenceText: Name of Product : CXDI-31

evidence.claim: Canon Inc. published the service manual and treats it as Canon property.
evidence.evidenceText: This service manual is published by Canon Inc.; This service manual is property of Canon Inc.

evidence.claim: The manual supports after-sales repair, installation understanding, specifications, technical information, parts ordering, and service updates.
evidence.evidenceText: This service manual belongs to a series of after-sales guides Canon Inc. publishes... Refer to “REPAIR GUIDE”... “PARTS CATALOG” and “TOOLS” for ordering parts and tools.

evidence.claim: Disassembly, assembly, adjustment, and maintenance are restricted to service persons trained by Canon.
evidence.evidenceText: Disassembly, assembly, adjustment and maintenance must be done only by a service person who has attended a service training designated by Canon.

evidence.claim: The manual instructs users not to modify the product because modification may result in fire or electric shock.
evidence.evidenceText: Never modify the product as it may result in fire or electric shock.

evidence.claim: For U.S.A., the equipment is stated to comply with Class A digital device limits under FCC Part 15.
evidence.evidenceText: This equipment has been tested and found to comply with the limits for a Class A digital device, pursuant to Part 15 of the FCC rules.
evidence.pageTarget: FCC Part 15 Class A Digital Device

evidence.claim: Shielded cable is required to comply with Class A limits in Subpart B of FCC Part 15.
evidence.evidenceText: Use of shielded cable is required to comply with class A limits in Subpart B of Part 15 of FCC rules.
evidence.pageTarget: FCC Part 15 Class A Digital Device

evidence.claim: For EU Countries, the instrument is marked as compliant with Directive 93/42/EEC.
evidence.evidenceText: The following mark shows compliance of the instrument with Directive 93/42/EEC.

evidence.claim: The instrument is classified as EN55011 Group 1/Class A.
evidence.evidenceText: This instrument has been classified into EN55011 Group 1/Class A.

evidence.claim: The instrument is Class I equipment according to EN 60601-1.
evidence.evidenceText: This instrument is a CLASS I EQUIPMENT according to EN 60601-1.

evidence.claim: The instrument is classified under EN60825-1:1994 as a Class 1 laser product.
evidence.evidenceText: This instrument has been classified under EN60825-1:1994 and conforms to the following classes: CLASS 1 LASER PRODUCT

evidence.claim: The E/O box is also classified under EN60825-1:1994 as a Class 1 laser product and under EN55011 Group 1/Class A.
evidence.evidenceText: The E/O box has been classified under EN60825-1:1994... CLASS 1 LASER PRODUCT... The E/O box has been classified into EN55011 Group 1/Class A.

evidence.claim: The CXDI-31 product configuration includes an imaging unit, E/O box, optical composite cable, E/O box power cable, storage box, connector guard parts, screw, and document set.
evidence.evidenceText: Digital X-ray camera CXDI-31 Configuration by item Quantity Imaging unit 1 E/O box 1 Optical composite cable 1 E/O box power cable 1 Storage box 1 ... Document set ... 1

evidence.claim: The CXDI-31 imaging unit uses a scintillator and amorphous silicon sensor described as a LANMIT sensor.
evidence.evidenceText: X-ray sensor Scintillator and Amorphous Silicon (a-Si) sensor LANMIT sensor

evidence.claim: The CXDI-31 effective filming range is 226 mm x 288 mm.
evidence.evidenceText: Effective filming range 226mm x 288mm

evidence.claim: The CXDI-31 pixel matrix size is 2256 x 2878 and the number of pixels is about 6.5 million.
evidence.evidenceText: Pixel matrix size 2256 x 2878 Unit pixel; No. of pixels About 6.5 million

evidence.claim: The CXDI-31 pixel size is 100 μm.
evidence.evidenceText: Pixel size 100 μm

evidence.claim: The CXDI-31 sensor output gradation is 16,384 14 bit and output gradation is 4,096 12 bit.
evidence.evidenceText: Sensor output gradation 16,384 14 bit; Output gradation 4,096 12 bit

evidence.claim: The CXDI-31 interface is DICOM3.0 over Ethernet via the control PC.
evidence.evidenceText: Interface DICOM3.0 (Ethernet) Via control PC

evidence.claim: The CXDI-31 imaging unit external dimensions are 324(W) x 327(L) x 20.3(H) mm and weight is 2.8 kg plus 0.5 kg cable.
evidence.evidenceText: External dimensions 324(W) x 327(L) x 20.3(H)mm; Weight 2.8kg (cable: 0.5kg)

evidence.claim: The E/O box external dimensions are 270(W) x 190(L) x 65(H) mm and weight is 2.2 kg.
evidence.evidenceText: External dimensions 270(W) x 190(L) x 65(H)mm; Weight 2.2 kg

evidence.claim: The optional CXDI-31 grid is fixed removable type with density 60/cm and grid ratios 4:1, 8:1, and 10:1.
evidence.evidenceText: Type Fixed type (removable); Density 60/cm; Grit ratio 4:1, 8:1, 10:1

evidence.claim: The CXDI-31 operating environment is +10 to +35°C and 30% to 75% humidity.
evidence.evidenceText: Operating environment Temperature: +10 - +35°C Humidity: 30% - 75%

evidence.claim: The CXDI-31 load limits are 150 kg uniform load over the sensor surface and 100 kg local load on a 40 mm diameter area.
evidence.evidenceText: Uniform Load 150kg over the whole area of sensor unit surface; Local Load 100kg on an area 40mm in diameter

reviewFlags.reason: Source metadata authoritySignal indicates MFDS/domestic, but the document text primarily provides Canon service information plus U.S.A./FCC and EU regulatory labeling; no MFDS authority content is present in the extracted text.
Distribution Control No. and Issued on fields are present but blank in the source text.
OCR quality is uneven in headings and some terms, with spaced characters and apparent typos such as 'Grit' for 'Grid' and duplicated/misnumbered procedure steps.
The source is a service manual rather than a regulatory certificate or approval letter; regulatory facts are limited to labels, classifications, warnings, and specifications stated in the manual.
